title: Local Image not found during build -  Buildah v1.19
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**

<!--
If I create an image locally on localhost in one Containerfile and then try to use it from a second Containerfile, the image isn't found by Buildah as it's not found in the list of registries.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
Run this script:

```
# cat ./test_on_build.sh
########
# Build Dockerfiles for OnBuild Test
# (Thanks @clcollins!)
########
FILE=./Dockerfile
/bin/cat <<EOM >$FILE
FROM alpine
RUN touch /foo
ONBUILD RUN touch /bar
EOM
chmod +x $FILE

FILE=./Dockerfile-2
/bin/cat <<EOM >$FILE
FROM onbuild-image
RUN touch /baz
EOM
chmod +x $FILE

########
# Build with Dockerfiles
########
buildah bud -f ./Dockerfile --format=docker -t onbuild-image .
buildah bud -f ./Dockerfile-2 --format=docker -t result-image .

########
# Build a container to see if the /bar file has been created.
########
ctr=$(buildah from result-image)

########
# Validate that the /bar file has been created in the container.
########
buildah run $ctr ls -alF /bar /foo /baz

```

The second `buildah bud` command fails as the onbuild-image is not in the list of repositories, it's in the local host:

```
[root@localhost ~]# buildah images
REPOSITORY                          TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED             SIZE
localhost/onbuild-image             latest   dc2e3e812ed1   3 minutes ago       5.85 MB
```

**Describe the results you received:**
```
# ./test_on_build.sh
STEP 1: FROM alpine
STEP 2: RUN touch /foo
STEP 3: ONBUILD RUN touch /bar
STEP 4: COMMIT onbuild-image
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 777b2c648970 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 431328ab6dbf done  
Copying config 2cb186bff2 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> 2cb186bff2b
2cb186bff2bafbefa591a6e08121ae05bc8e1032cb0faa395483aee7e1f8eaec
STEP 1: FROM onbuild-image
? Please select an image: 
  ▸ registry.fedoraproject.org/onbuild-image:latest
    registry.access.redhat.com/onbuild-image:latest
    registry.centos.org/onbuild-image:latest
    docker.io/library/onbuild-image:latest
 ```
Hitting return causes an image not found error for any of these.

**Describe the results you expected:**

I at least expected a localhost/onbuild-image to be one of the selectable images and then successful completion.

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
]# rpm -q buildah
buildah-1.19.0-1.fc32.x86_64

```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
# buildah version
Version:         1.19.0
Go Version:      go1.14.13
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  
image Version:   5.9.0
Git Commit:      
Built:           Wed Dec 31 19:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64

```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
# cat /etc/*release
Fedora release 32 (Thirty Two)
NAME=Fedora
VERSION="32 (Server Edition)"
ID=fedora
VERSION_ID=32
VERSION_CODENAME=""
PLATFORM_ID="platform:f32"
PRETTY_NAME="Fedora 32 (Server Edition)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;34"
LOGO=fedora-logo-icon
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:32"
HOME_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/"
DOCUMENTATION_URL="https://docs.fedoraproject.org/en-US/fedora/f32/system-administrators-guide/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Communicating_and_getting_help"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=32
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION=32
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Legal:PrivacyPolicy"
VARIANT="Server Edition"
VARIANT_ID=server
Fedora release 32 (Thirty Two)
Fedora release 32 (Thirty Two)
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
# uname -a
Linux localhost.localdomain 5.8.11-200.fc32.x86_64 #1 SMP Wed Sep 23 13:51:28 UTC 2020 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

comments.body: @vrothberg PTAL.  If this is failing as I think it is, we'll need to fix, get a 1.19.1 together and update Podman 3.0 (maybe 2.2.1 too?)

Tried one other thing, if you set 

`FROM onbuild-image`

to

`FROM localhost/onbuild-image`

it works.  This lessens the severity, but it will still break people I fear.
Yes this is a serious bug, and should be fixed ASAP
Is this a Buildah bug though?  Should we have searched through containers/storage to see if localhost/onbuild-image exists before prompting?

From a very quick skim, the https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/cfb3372985533411fe91249c5162e6a82a13fe20/new.go#L148-L162 code path _should_ try pre-pending `localhost` (via `util.ResolveName`+`shortnames.ResolveLocally`). I can’t immediately see how that fails.
As @mtrmac mentioned, the `shortnames.ResolveLocally` is already using `localhost`.  I _think_ it's a Buildah bug specific to the `FROM` code path.
Aaah, I see it now.  It relates to the pull-policy logic.  I'll wrap up a fix.
